### Post by fred the wise on 2007-06-28
Hi all!
I've got problems with my filesystem; resizing my ext3 partition qtparted gives me an error message (!);

this is my etc/fstab:
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0

# Entry for /dev/hda3 :

UUID=6ca67407-4dc8-40a1-950a-2e955cd87b4b / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1

/dev/hda1 /media/windows vfat iocharset=utf8, umask=000 0 0

# Entry for /dev/hda2 :

UUID=43eccb56-ca21-4ef9-b70f-f0071f5d1e2b none swap sw 0 0
```

and if I run fdisk -l:
```
   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/hda1   *           1        1364    10956298+   c  W95 FAT32 (LBA)
/dev/hda2            1365        1390      208845   82  Linux swap / Solaris
/dev/hda3            1391        3648    18137385   83  Linux
```

I find that the windows partition is the boot one.

then..when I run the filesystem check at startup it appears the message: " warning..utf8 is not a recommended IO charset for FAT filesystems..."...

I can't obtain information about how to re-estabilish the integral filesystem and so resize and move my partitions without problems on forums...
THKS
Fred_

### Post by smoker on 2007-06-28
hi, i'm not an expert on this, but i think i would try running a windows chkdsk /f /r on your fat32 partition.

best of luck

### Post by fred the wise on 2007-06-28
well the problem is i can't resize nor move my partition (fat32 and ext3) because i'm not able to unmount them...the fat32 is the only partition active... :(

### Post by Inxsible on 2007-06-28
> **fred the wise said:**
> Hi all!
I've got problems with my filesystem; resizing my ext3 partition qtparted gives me an error message (!);
 
this is my etc/fstab:
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>
 
proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
 
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
 
UUID=6ca67407-4dc8-40a1-950a-2e955cd87b4b / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
 
/dev/hda1 /media/windows vfat [COLOR=red]iocharset=utf8[/COLOR], umask=000 0 0
 
# Entry for /dev/hda2 :
 
UUID=43eccb56-ca21-4ef9-b70f-f0071f5d1e2b none swap sw 0 0
```
 
and if I run fdisk -l:
```
   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/hda1   *           1        1364    10956298+   c  W95 FAT32 (LBA)
/dev/hda2            1365        1390      208845   82  Linux swap / Solaris
/dev/hda3            1391        3648    18137385   83  Linux
```
 
I find that the windows partition is the boot one.
 
then..when I run the filesystem check at startup it appears the message: " warning..utf8 is not a recommended IO charset for FAT filesystems..."...
 
I can't obtain information about how to re-estabilish the integral filesystem and so resize and move my partitions without problems on forums...
THKS
Fred_
 
The error is quite explanatory. From your fstab, remove what I marked in Red

---

### Post by fred the wise on 2007-06-28
ah...as you see:
i cannot move my ext3 partition (it's in the end of my HD).. i have to copy my data and then create a new one...

---

### Post by fred the wise on 2007-06-29
Ok I've solved it:
i removed the "iocharset=utf8" but I had to defrag and resize the clusters of the fat32 partition..
now it's better..:D
thank you

---
